name: Payers and Health Insurance Subpack
description: Vertical-specialized value intelligence for payers and health insurance organizations covering commercial plans, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid managed care, employer plans, TPAs, PBMs, dental/vision, stop-loss, and VBC/ACOs. Extends Healthcare Master ValuePack with payer-specific pains, KPIs, formulas, benchmarks, signals, personas, discovery questions, objections, technology systems, and regulatory factors.

pains.name: Medicare Advantage Star Rating Erosion
pains.description: Star rating below 4.0 or declining year-over-year, threatening Quality Bonus Payment (QBP), rebate retention, and marketing rights. Root causes span HEDIS measure gaps, CAHPS underperformance, medication adherence shortfalls, and HOS decline.

pains.name: Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) Compression Below 85%
pains.description: MLR exceeding 85-87% with administrative costs above industry median, squeezing competitive pricing ability and threatening regulatory compliance. Driven by medical cost trend acceleration, reinsurance cost increases, COVID-era morbidity unwind, and new market entrant premium pressure.
symptoms: Competitive premium pressure from new entrants

pains.name: Claims Auto-Adjudication Gap and Backlog
pains.description: Auto-adjudication rate below 85% with claims backlog exceeding 10 days, driving manual processing costs, provider abrasion, and member dissatisfaction. Root causes include benefits configuration complexity, provider data quality, and outdated rules engines.
symptoms: Manual touch rate > 15% for routine claims

pains.name: Fraud Waste Abuse (FWA) Detection Gap
pains.description: FWA detection rate below 1% of claims with recovery ratios below 3:1, leaving significant financial recovery on the table and exposing the organization to DOJ investigation and qui tam risk.
symptoms: DOJ settlement history or qui tam exposure

pains.name: Care Gap Closure and HEDIS Underperformance
pains.description: Care gap closure rate below 60% with HEDIS measure gaps in 5+ measures, threatening Stars bonus, quality withhold recovery, and member health outcomes. Driven by data fragmentation, outreach response limitations, and hybrid measure reporting complexity.

pains.name: Risk Adjustment Revenue Leakage and RADV Exposure
pains.description: RAF score gap exceeding 0.08 vs. expected with HCC recapture below 85%, causing underpayment from CMS and exposing the organization to RADV extrapolated penalties. Root causes include suspecting tool underutilization, physician coding gaps, and EMR integration failures.
symptoms: Retrospective chart review yield < $500 per chart
ICD-10 coding specificity gaps in 3+ HCC categories

pains.name: Medicare Advantage Member Churn & Retention Crisis
pains.description: Voluntary disenrollment rate exceeding 12% annually with high broker-driven churn, threatening member lifetime value (LTV) and plan viability. Driven by NPS deficits, digital engagement gaps, and broker commission competition.

pains.name: Medicaid MCO Margin Compression & Rate Pressure
pains.description: State rate increases lagging medical cost trends by 2+ percentage points with MLR exceeding 90%, making Medicaid MCO margins unsustainable. Driven by high-cost member pool growth (LTSS, I/DD, SMI), encounter data quality issues, and retroactive rate adjustments.
sources: CMS Medicaid Managed Care Financial Report

pains.name: Employer Group RFP Loss & Commercial Risk Erosion
pains.description: Employer group loss ratio exceeding 80% with RFP win rates below 30%, driving commercial risk book erosion and self-insured migration. Driven by premium increase requests exceeding 8%, broker defection, and direct primary care carve-outs.
symptoms: Employer group loss ratio > 80% for > 2 years
Premium increase requests > 8% driving broker defection
Self-insured ASO migration > 10% of risk book
sources: Mercer National Survey of Employer-Sponsored Health Plans

pains.name: PBM Drug Cost Escalation & Rebate Transparency Pressure
pains.description: Specialty drug spend exceeding 45% of pharmacy budget with rebate retention under legislative pressure, GDR below 84%, and biosimilar adoption lagging. DIR fee accumulation and accumulator/maximizer program member complaints add regulatory and reputational risk.
symptoms: Specialty drug spend > 45% of pharmacy budget
Rebate retention under pressure (PBM reform legislation)
Accumulator / maximizer program member complaints increasing

pains.name: Network Adequacy & Provider Contracting Disputes
pains.description: Network adequacy complaints exceeding CMS/state thresholds with OON rates above 8%, provider dispute rates above 5%, and No Surprises Act arbitration backlog. Driven by provider rate inflation, narrow network legal challenges, and directory accuracy mandates.
symptoms: Provider dispute rate > 5% of in-network claims
sources: No Surprises Act Federal IDR Reports

pains.name: Stop-Loss Premium Escalation & LASER Proliferation
pains.description: Stop-loss premium trend exceeding 12% annually with LASER rates above 15% of covered employees and aggregate attachment point breaches increasing. Driven by high-cost claimant frequency, specialty drug inflation, and specific deductible escalation.

pains.name: Grievances & Appeals Backlog / State DOI Complaints
pains.description: Grievance resolution time exceeding 30 days with appeal overturn rates above 40% and state DOI complaints above industry median. Creates CMS sanction risk, CMP exposure, and reputational damage.
symptoms: State DOI complaints per 1,000 members > industry median
CMS sanctions or CMP for untimely determinations
Member call escalations > 15% of total volume

pains.name: Value-Based Contracting Performance & Shared Savings Shortfall
pains.description: ACO shared savings rate below 50% of target with total cost of care trend exceeding benchmark by 2+ percentage points. Driven by quality measure attainment gaps, provider administrative burden, attribution disputes, and downside risk exposure.
symptoms: ACO shared savings rate < 50% of target
Total cost of care (TCC) trend > benchmark + 2%

pains.name: Digital Member Engagement & Portal Underutilization
pains.description: Member portal registration below 40% with mobile app MAU below 15% of members and telehealth utilization below 5% of eligible encounters. Drives higher call center costs, lower care gap closure rates, and CAHPS information measure underperformance.
symptoms: Digital care gap closure rate < 20% of total
CAHPS health plan information measure < 60th percentile
sources: J.D. Power U.S. Commercial Member Health Plan Study 2024

pains.name: COB / Subrogation Recovery Gap
pains.description: COB recovery rate below 60% of identified overpayments with subrogation identification below 50% of accident-related claims. Driven by other insurance verification gaps, MSP compliance errors, and conditional payment recovery delays.
symptoms: COB recovery rate < 60% of identified overpayments
Subrogation identification rate < 50% of accident-related claims
Other insurance verification < 70% at point of service
MSP compliance errors > 5% of eligible claims

pains.name: Pharmacy DIR Fee & Reimbursement Timing Compression
pains.description: DIR fee drag exceeding $12 PMPM with retroactive DIR adjustments above $5M annually. Driven by CMS True OOP / counting rule changes, pharmacy reimbursement compression, and 340B contract pharmacy exclusion impacts.
symptoms: CMS True OOP / counting rule uncertainty

pains.name: Medicare Advantage RADV Audit and Risk Adjustment Compliance Exposure
pains.description: RADV extrapolation error rate exceeding 10% with condition-specific documentation gaps and HCC invalidation rates above 15% on audit. Creates conditional payment demands, extrapolated repayments, and Star rating impact.
symptoms: Condition-specific documentation gaps in HCC categories 87/88/96/111

kpis.name: Medicare Advantage Star Rating
kpis.formula: CMS weighted composite of 40+ measures across 5 domains

kpis.name: HEDIS Hybrid Measure Rate
kpis.formula: (HEDIS Hybrid Measures Met / Total Applicable Hybrid Measures) × 100

kpis.name: Medication Adherence (PDC)
kpis.formula: Days Covered by Supply / Days in Period (diabetes, HTN, cholesterol)

kpis.name: CAHPS Health Plan Score
kpis.formula: Medicare CAHPS / Commercial CAHPS composite score

kpis.name: Medical Loss Ratio (MLR)
kpis.formula: (Medical Claims + QI Expenses) / Total Premium Revenue × 100

kpis.name: Administrative Cost PMPM
kpis.formula: Total Administrative Expenses / Total Member Months

kpis.name: SG&A as % of Premium Revenue
kpis.formula: SG&A Expenses / Total Premium Revenue × 100

kpis.name: Claims Auto-Adjudication Rate
kpis.formula: (Claims Auto-Adjudicated / Total Claims Received) × 100

kpis.name: Average Claims Processing Time
kpis.formula: Sum of Days from Receipt to Adjudication / Total Claims Processed

kpis.name: First-Pass Resolution Rate
kpis.formula: (Claims Paid Correctly on First Submission / Total Claims Submitted) × 100

kpis.name: FWA Detection Rate
kpis.formula: (Identified FWA Claims / Total Claims Processed) × 100

kpis.name: FWA Recovery Ratio
kpis.formula: Total FWA Recoveries / Total SIU Operating Costs

kpis.name: Prepay Detection Rate
kpis.formula: (FWA Claims Stopped Prepayment / Total FWA Identified) × 100

kpis.name: Care Gap Closure Rate
kpis.formula: (Closed Care Gaps / Total Identified Care Gaps) × 100

kpis.name: Preventable ED Utilization Rate
kpis.formula: (Preventable ED Visits / Total Member Months) × 1,000

kpis.name: HEDIS Measure Attainment
kpis.formula: (Measures Meeting Target / Total Applicable Measures) × 100

kpis.name: Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) Score
kpis.formula: Sum of CMS-HCC Risk Scores / Total Enrollees

kpis.name: HCC Recapture Rate
kpis.formula: (HCCs Documented Current Year / Total Prior Year HCCs) × 100

kpis.name: Suspecting Rate
kpis.formula: (Encounters with HCC-Suspecting Documentation / Total Eligible Encounters) × 100

kpis.name: Voluntary Disenrollment Rate
kpis.formula: (Voluntary Disenrollments / Average Member Count) × 100

kpis.name: Member NPS
kpis.formula: Net Promoter Score from member satisfaction survey

kpis.name: Digital Engagement Rate
kpis.formula: (Active Digital Users / Total Members) × 100

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Star rating < 4.0 with HEDIS gap in 3+ domains
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Quality underperformance threatening QBP bonus

valueDrivers.signalPattern: CAHPS score < 75th percentile with churn > 12%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Member experience driving disenrollment and LTV loss

valueDrivers.signalPattern: MLR > 87% with admin PMPM > $65
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Administrative cost burden eroding competitive pricing

valueDrivers.signalPattern: SG&A > 12% with auto-adjudication < 85%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Operational inefficiency in claims driving admin bloat

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Auto-adjudication < 85% with backlog > 10 days
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Manual claims processing creating cost and provider abrasion

valueDrivers.signalPattern: First-pass resolution < 90% with call volume increasing
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Rework loop consuming FTEs and member satisfaction

valueDrivers.signalPattern: FWA detection < 1% with recovery < 3:1

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Prepay detection < 25% with false positive > 90%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Post-pay recovery model is expensive and provider-abrasive

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Care gap closure < 60% with preventable ED > 100/1K
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Unmanaged chronic disease driving medical cost trend
requiredEvidence: Care gap closure trend by measure

valueDrivers.signalPattern: HEDIS attainment < 70% with Stars < 4.0
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Quality measure failure blocking bonus and retention

valueDrivers.signalPattern: RAF gap > 0.08 with recapture < 85%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Under-documentation causing underpayment vs. CMS benchmark

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Suspecting rate < 40% with EMR tool underutilization
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Missed encounter opportunities for HCC capture

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Churn > 15% with broker-driven > 40%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Distribution instability threatening membership base

valueDrivers.signalPattern: NPS < 20 with digital engagement < 20%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Member experience and digital maturity lagging competitors

valueDrivers.signalPattern: State rate increase < medical trend + 2% with MLR > 90%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Medicaid MCO margin unsustainable without intervention

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Encounter rejection > 15% with retroactive adjustments > $5M
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Encounter data quality undermining state reporting and payment

valueDrivers.signalPattern: RFP win rate < 30% with self-insured migration > 10%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Commercial risk book erosion threatening top-line

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Premium increase > 8% with broker defection > 5%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Pricing pressure creating retention spiral

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Specialty spend > 45% with GDR < 84%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Drug mix shift toward high-cost specialty without generic optimization

valueDrivers.signalPattern: DIR fee drag > $12 PMPM with retroactive adjustments > $5M
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Pharmacy reimbursement timing and fee structure compressing margin

valueDrivers.signalPattern: OON rate > 8% with directory accuracy < 85%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Network adequacy and accuracy issues driving regulatory risk

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Provider dispute rate > 5% with arbitration backlog
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Contracting friction increasing cost and abrasion

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Stop-loss premium trend > 12% with LASER > 15%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Underwriting precision declining; high-cost claimant identification poor

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Aggregate attachment breach frequency increasing with specific > $500K
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Large claim management and reinsurance placement suboptimal

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Grievance resolution > 30 days with overturn rate > 40%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Determination quality issues creating compliance and cost exposure

valueDrivers.signalPattern: State DOI complaints > median with CMS sanctions history
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Regulatory escalation threatening license and contract eligibility

valueDrivers.signalPattern: ACO shared savings < 50% of target with TCC trend > benchmark + 2%
valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Value-based contract underperformance eroding provider trust

valueDrivers.interpretedPain: Digital maturity gap increasing service cost and member dissatisfaction

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Telehealth utilization < 5% with CAHPS information < 60th percentile

formulas.name: MA Stars Bonus Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Rating Improvement × Member Months × Bonus PMPM) + QBP Enhancement + Rebate Retention
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when using CMS published QBP tables; MEDIUM when projecting member months

formulas.name: MLR Admin Cost Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: (Current Admin PMPM – Target) × Member Months + (SG&A Reduction × Premium Revenue)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when based on internal GL; MEDIUM when using industry benchmarks

formulas.name: Claims Auto-Adjudication Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Volume × Manual Cost per Claim × Automation Lift %) + (Backlog Reduction × Interest Cost) + (Provider Satisfaction Impact on Network Cost)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when vendor provides automated measurement; MEDIUM for provider satisfaction quantification

formulas.name: FWA Recovery Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Prepay Stopped × Avg Claim Value) + (Postpay Recovery × Recovery Rate) – (SIU Operating Cost × (1 + Target ROI))
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when SIU costs are tracked; MEDIUM for prepay valuation

formulas.name: Care Gap Closure Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Gaps Closed × Avg Cost per Gap) + (Preventable ED Avoided × Avg ED Cost) + (Stars Improvement × Bonus PMPM × Member Months)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when linked to HEDIS/Stars; MEDIUM for standalone commercial

formulas.name: RAF Optimization Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Target RAF – Current RAF) × Member Months × Benchmark Rate × Coding Adjustment – RADV Penalty Risk
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when retrospective chart review data available; MEDIUM for suspecting-only programs

formulas.name: Member Retention Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Churn Reduction × Member Months × LTV per Member) + (Broker Churn Reduction × Acquisition Cost Savings)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when LTV model exists; MEDIUM when using industry LTV estimates

formulas.name: Medicaid MCO Margin Protection
formulas.formulaExpression: (Encounter Rejection Reduction × Avg Claim Value × 12) + (Rate Appeal Success × Retroactive Adjustment $) + (Administrative Cap Efficiency)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when state reporting exists; MEDIUM for rate appeal projections

formulas.name: Employer Group Retention Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Group Retention Improvement × Group Premium) + (RFP Win Rate Improvement × Pipeline Value × Win Probability)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when pipeline CRM data available; MEDIUM for win probability

formulas.name: PBM Drug Trend Management
formulas.formulaExpression: (Specialty Trend Reduction × Specialty Spend) + (Biosimilar Adoption Increase × Brand Spend) + (DIR Fee Reduction × Part D Membership)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when PBM contract data available; MEDIUM for trend attribution

formulas.name: Network Adequacy & Dispute Cost Avoidance
formulas.formulaExpression: (Dispute Reduction × Avg Dispute Resolution Cost) + (No Surprises Act Arbitration Avoidance × Avg Arbitration Cost) + (Directory Accuracy Improvement × Member Complaint Cost)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when legal department tracks; MEDIUM for complaint cost quantification

formulas.name: Grievance & Appeals Efficiency Value
formulas.formulaExpression: (Resolution Time Reduction × FTE Cost Savings) + (Overturn Rate Reduction × Avg Overturn Cost) + (CMP Avoidance × Probability)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when compliance tracks; MEDIUM for CMP probability

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: MA plan declines 0.5+ stars or below 4.0
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Quality underperformance threatening QBP and marketing rights

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: NAIC filing shows admin cost PMPM increase > 15% YoY
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Administrative bloat or operational crisis pressuring MLR compliance

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CMS issues sanctions, CMP, or marketing suspension
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Compliance failure with direct financial and operational penalties

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Quarterly enrollment flat or declining vs. market growth
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Product-market fit or broker relationship deterioration

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: NAIC complaint index > 1.5x median for 2+ quarters
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Service quality or claims handling issues escalating to regulators

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: State awards contract to competitor or reduces service area
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Revenue and margin pressure from core government book

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PBM client issues RFP or announces non-renewal
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Rebate transparency or service quality pressure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Stop-loss carrier reports claimant exceeding specific deductible
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Laser rate pressure and aggregate attachment risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CMS issues RADV audit or conditional payment demand
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Documentation quality failure with extrapolation risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Employer announces plan switch or self-insured migration
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Commercial risk erosion and pricing competitiveness gap

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: DOJ unseals qui tam or announces settlement
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Systematic fraud detection or coding practice failure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: > 5% of network providers terminated or not renewed
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Network adequacy risk and member disruption

signalRules.signalName: HEDIS Audit Finding / NCQA Accreditation Risk
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: NCQA issues deferral or HEDIS audit finding
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Quality reporting integrity at risk; Stars/HEDIS measure jeopardy

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Plan submits CMS comment on DIR / True OOP / counting rules
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Pharmacy reimbursement timing and fee structure uncertainty

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: RFP for member portal, app, or CRM platform
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Digital maturity gap driving service cost or member dissatisfaction

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: MSSP or commercial ACO misses savings target
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Value-based contract underperformance eroding provider relationships

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Job postings or RFP for COB/subrogation services
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Recovery gap or MSP compliance concern

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Job postings for in-house risk adjustment or vendor RFP
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: RAF optimization pressure or RADV response need

personas.name: Chief Medical Officer (Plan)
dislikedClaims: One size fits all care management programs

personas.name: Network Contracting Director
dislikedClaims: Network optimization that means access reduction
Value-based contracts without shared savings evidence
Arbitrary rate cuts without quality linkage

personas.name: Grievances & Appeals Manager
trustedEvidence: CMS Medicare Advantage compliance activity reports
dislikedClaims: Automation replacing clinical judgment in determinations

personas.name: Population Health Analyst
dislikedClaims: Care gap lists without outreach integration
Population health analytics without closed-loop workflow

buyingTriggers.name: CMS Star Rating Decline Below 4.0
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS releases Star Ratings showing plan below 4.0 or declining
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Board-level priority; quality vendor selection; 6-9 month implementation

buyingTriggers.name: MLR Filing Shows Admin Cost Spike
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: NAIC MLR filing reveals admin cost increase threatening compliance
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: CFO-driven; operational efficiency RFP; 3-6 month decision

buyingTriggers.name: CMS Sanctions or CMP Notice
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS issues sanctions, CMP, or marketing suspension notice
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compliance-driven; limited vendor pool; accelerated procurement

buyingTriggers.name: MA Member Growth Plateau or Decline
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Quarterly enrollment flat or declining versus market growth

buyingTriggers.name: State DOI Complaint Index Surge
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: NAIC complaint index exceeds 1.5x median for 2+ quarters

buyingTriggers.name: Medicaid MCO Contract Rebid or Loss
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: State awards contract to competitor or reduces service area

buyingTriggers.name: PBM Contract RFP or Non-Renewal
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: PBM client issues RFP or announces non-renewal
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Procurement-driven; pricing transparency focus; 12-18 month cycle

buyingTriggers.name: High-Cost Claimant / LASER Event
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Stop-loss carrier reports claimant exceeding specific deductible
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Underwriting-driven; clinical management vendor; 3-6 month cycle

buyingTriggers.name: RADV Audit Letter or Conditional Payment Demand
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS issues RADV audit or conditional payment demand
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compliance-driven; risk adjustment vendor; 1-3 month accelerated

buyingTriggers.name: Employer Group Defection or Self-Insured Migration
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Employer announces plan switch or self-insured migration
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: CSO/Sales-driven; pricing and service platform; 6-12 month cycle

buyingTriggers.name: FWA Whistleblower or DOJ Investigation
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: DOJ unseals qui tam or announces settlement
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Legal-driven; SIU technology and services; 1-3 month accelerated

buyingTriggers.name: Network Provider Termination Crisis
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: > 5% of network providers terminated or not renewed
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Network/CSO-driven; directory and adequacy platform; 3-6 month cycle

buyingTriggers.name: HEDIS Audit Finding or NCQA Deferral
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: NCQA issues deferral or HEDIS audit finding
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Quality-driven; HEDIS/quality reporting platform; 3-6 month cycle

buyingTriggers.name: ACO Shared Savings Miss or Provider Exit
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: MSSP or commercial ACO misses savings target
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: VBC-driven; population health and analytics platform; 6-9 month cycle

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current Medicare Advantage Star rating, and which three measures moved most year-over-year?
Walk me through your MLR trend over the last three years. What is your admin cost PMPM vs. premium trend?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Margin compression severity and admin cost opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your auto-adjudication rate, and what percentage of claims require manual touch for non-complex reasons?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Operational efficiency gap and automation potential

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you identify and close HCC coding gaps? What is your recapture rate, RAF trend, and current RADV status?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: RAF optimization opportunity and audit exposure

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your voluntary disenrollment rate, and what percentage is broker-driven vs. member-initiated?
What was your last state rate increase for Medicaid, and how does it compare to your medical cost trend?
What is your employer group RFP win rate, and what are the top three reasons cited for losses?
How is your PBM managing specialty drug trend, and what is your current DIR fee impact on Part D?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Pharmacy cost management and fee transparency

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your network adequacy complaint rate, and how many No Surprises Act arbitrations are pending?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: Network regulatory exposure and dispute volume

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your stop-loss LASER rate, and how has your high-cost claimant frequency trended over three years?
What is your average grievance resolution time, and what is your appeal overturn rate by level?
How are your ACO shared savings performing vs. target? What is your total cost of care trend vs. benchmark?
discoveryQuestions.insightSought: VBC contract performance and provider trust

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your digital member engagement rate (portal, app, telehealth), and how does it correlate with satisfaction?
What is your COB recovery rate and subrogation identification rate? How much recovery is left on the table?
What is your FWA detection rate, false positive rate, and recovery ratio? How much is prepay vs. postpay?
How are you preparing for the CMS-HCC Model V28 transition? What is your expected RAF impact?
What is your HEDIS hybrid measure reporting process, and what was your last NCQA audit outcome?
What are your top three strategic initiatives for the next 18 months, and how will technology enable them?

objections.objection: We already have a PBM/vendor that handles pharmacy
objections.underlyingConcern: Fear of disruption, rebate loss, contract complexity
objections.reframeStrategy: Position as complementary analytics layer; demonstrate PBM-agnostic value; reference PBM clients

objections.objection: Our Stars score is good enough; we don't need change
objections.reframeStrategy: Quantify QBP at risk from measure drift; show competitor improvement; reference 0.1 star = $X PMPM

objections.objection: The state sets our rates; we can't control margin
objections.underlyingConcern: Fatalism about Medicaid economics, resistance to operational solutions
objections.reframeStrategy: Reframe to encounter efficiency, care management ROI, administrative cap optimization; show state MCO peers

objections.objection: Our actuaries need to validate any financial projection
objections.underlyingConcern: Skepticism of vendor ROI claims, professional risk aversion
objections.reframeStrategy: Provide actuarial-ready models with confidence intervals; offer actuary-to-actuary review; reference actuarial firm validations

objections.objection: We tried risk adjustment and it didn't move the needle
objections.underlyingConcern: Past vendor failure, EMR integration trauma, physician resistance
objections.reframeStrategy: Diagnose prior failure (suspecting vs. in-home assessment vs. coding education); differentiate with closed-loop workflow; offer pilot with yield guarantee

objections.objection: Our network team handles contracting; we don't need help
objections.underlyingConcern: Territory protection, skepticism of external benchmarks
objections.reframeStrategy: Position as data and analytics augmentation, not replacement; offer FAIR Health benchmark access; show dispute prediction value

objections.objection: Grievances and appeals is a compliance function, not a tech function
objections.underlyingConcern: Fear of automation replacing compliance judgment
objections.reframeStrategy: Emphasize workflow automation and tracking, not determination automation; show CMS sanction avoidance value; reference compliant deployments

objections.objection: We don't have budget for a new platform this year
objections.reframeStrategy: Frame as self-funding via MLR reduction or recovery increase; phased implementation; success fee model

objections.objection: Our data is too fragmented across claims, EMR, and pharmacy
objections.underlyingConcern: Data integration anxiety, past project failure
objections.reframeStrategy: Include data readiness assessment; demonstrate entity resolution and master data capabilities; reference fragmented environment success

technologySystems.name: Core Administration Platform (Claims)
technologySystems.description: Policy admin, claims adjudication, benefits configuration, enrollment management

technologySystems.name: FWA Detection & SIU Platform
technologySystems.description: Prepay and postpay fraud detection, predictive analytics, case management, investigative workflow

technologySystems.name: Risk Adjustment / HCC Suspecting Engine
technologySystems.description: RAF optimization, HCC recapture tracking, suspecting algorithms, chart review workflow, coding education

technologySystems.name: HEDIS / Quality Reporting Engine
technologySystems.description: HEDIS measure calculation, hybrid measure data collection, NCQA submission, Stars simulation

technologySystems.name: Member Engagement / CRM Platform
technologySystems.description: Member portal, mobile app, omnichannel outreach, digital care gap closure, preference management

technologySystems.name: Provider Directory & Network Management
technologySystems.description: Provider data management, credentialing, network adequacy monitoring, directory accuracy, contract management

technologySystems.name: Value-Based Care / ACO Analytics
technologySystems.description: Shared savings calculation, total cost of care trending, attribution logic, provider scorecards, VBC contract modeling

technologySystems.name: PBM / Pharmacy Management System
technologySystems.description: Formulary management, claims adjudication, rebate administration, DIR tracking, specialty pharmacy

technologySystems.name: Grievances & Appeals Management
technologySystems.description: Grievance intake, tracking, determination workflow, appeal escalation, regulatory reporting, IRO coordination

technologySystems.name: COB / Subrogation Recovery System
technologySystems.description: Other insurance identification, MSP compliance, subrogation case management, recovery tracking, conditional payment

technologySystems.name: Prior Authorization Automation
technologySystems.description: PA intake, clinical decision support, auto-approval pathways, provider portal, turnaround tracking

technologySystems.name: Actuarial & Pricing Workbench
technologySystems.description: Trend analysis, reserve modeling, rating development, MLR simulation, bid optimization, VBC financial modeling

regulatoryFactors.name: ACA Section 1557 (Nondiscrimination)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All health plans receiving federal funds
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: OCR investigation; CMP; contract termination risk

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Star Ratings (42 CFR 422.166)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All MA and Part D plans
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Annual Oct release; measurement year prior
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Bonus loss $50-150 PMPM; marketing restriction; contract termination

regulatoryFactors.name: MLR Reporting (ACA 2718 / 45 CFR 158)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Commercial, MA, Part D, Medicaid MCOs
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Rebate obligation to enrollees/state; CMS corrective action

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS Medicare Advantage & Part D Final Rule (CY 2025+)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Marketing suspension; CMP; Star rating reduction; contract non-renewal

regulatoryFactors.name: State DOI / Medicaid Agency Reporting
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Contract termination; CMP; restriction on new business

regulatoryFactors.name: No Surprises Act (NSA) / Federal Independent Dispute Resolution
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Plans covering OON emergency and air ambulance; IDR entities
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Federal CMP; arbitration award; state enforcement

regulatoryFactors.name: CMS RADV (Risk Adjustment Data Validation)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Extrapolated repayment; conditional payment demand; Star rating impact

regulatoryFactors.name: PBM Transparency / FTC 6(b) Study
regulatoryFactors.applicability: PBMs; health plans with insourced PBM
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: FTC enforcement; state PBM licensure action; contract ineligibility

regulatoryFactors.name: HIPAA / HITECH (45 CFR 160, 164)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All covered entities and business associates
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $137-$2.068M per category; criminal up to $250K + 10 years; OCR settlement

competitorFactors.competitorFactor: UnitedHealth / Optum Vertical Integration (payer + provider + PBM + data)
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Independent payers must differentiate on provider neutrality, data portability, or specialty network depth
Accelerates PBM-medical integration; drives medical cost management and pharmacy trend collaboration
Employer market consolidation; drives whole-health and behavioral health bundling; independent vendors must show integration value
MA market concentration; drives provider-primary care integration; competitors seek clinical differentiation

competitorFactors.competitorFactor: Oscar Health / Clover Health / Devoted Health (Tech-Native MA)
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Digital-first member experience and risk adjustment sophistication pressures legacy plans to modernize
Medicaid market concentration; state procurements favor scale; niche players serve carve-outs (LTSS, behavioral health)

competitorFactors.competitorFactor: Amazon Pharmacy / One Medical / PillPack Entry
competitorFactors.impactOnBuyingBehavior: Employer and MA pharmacy cost transparency pressure; drives PBM RFP activity and rebate scrutiny
State procurements favor scale; niche players serve supplements or specialized populations

workedExamples.name: Medicare Advantage Star Rating Recovery
workedExamples.context: Regional MA plan with 180,000 members dropped from 4.0 to 3.5 stars. QBP bonus at risk = $85 PMPM.

calculation.implementationCost: $18M (quality programs, HEDIS data collection, CAHPS intervention)

workedExamples.validationRequired: Confirm current HEDIS measure-level gaps; validate CAHPS improvement feasibility; assess whether 0.5 star is achievable in single measurement year or requires multi-year trajectory.

workedExamples.name: Medicaid MCO Operational Efficiency
workedExamples.context: State Medicaid MCO with 400,000 members. MLR at 91%, admin cap at 4.8%, encounter rejection rate at 18%.
workedExamples.validationRequired: Confirm state encounter acceptance criteria; validate encounter volume and rejection root causes; assess state timeline for rate reconsideration.

workedExamples.name: Commercial Employer Group Retention + PBM Optimization
workedExamples.context: Commercial health plan losing employer groups to self-insured migration. 50,000 risk lives at risk. PBM specialty trend at 16% with DIR fee drag at $14 PMPM.

calculation.groupRetention: Prevent loss of 15K lives × $450 PMPM × 12 = $81M premium retention

workedExamples.validationRequired: Confirm employer contract renewal calendar; validate PBM contract terms (rebate guarantees, DIR pass-through); assess biosimilar formulary positioning.

inheritedComponents: Value Driver Framework (Revenue Uplift / Cost Savings / Risk Reduction / Working Capital)
Base Persona Archetypes (CFO, COO, Chief Strategy Officer, CISO, CCO, VP Revenue Cycle, HIM Director, Patient Access Director)
Evidence Source Types (10-K, CMS data, earnings calls, job postings, breach portals, bond ratings, industry surveys)
Signal Source Taxonomy (financial, operational, regulatory, competitive)
Benchmark Methodology (industry survey, government, financial ratings, academic)
Governance Framework (confidence levels, validation rules, approval gates)

Pains P006 (Prior Authorization Burden), P008 (MA Risk Adjustment Gap), P010 (Claims Adjudication Backlog), P011 (MA Star Rating Erosion), P012 (MLR Pressure), P017 (Cybersecurity / HIPAA Breach), P021 (FWA Detection Gap), P022 (Care Gap Closure)
KPIs K021-K023 (PA metrics), K027-K029 (RAF metrics), K033-K042 (Claims/Stars/MLR metrics), K068-K073 (FWA/Care Gap metrics)
Value Drivers V015 (PA burden), V019 (RAF gap), V025 (Star rating < 4.0), V029 (Trial enrollment)
Signal Rules S005 (CMS Star decline), S012 (Medicaid MCO contract loss), S018 (Cybersecurity incident), S030 (C-suite change)
Tech Systems T016 (Claims Management), T017 (FWA Detection)
Regulatory REG001 (HIPAA), REG003 (MA Star Ratings), REG004 (ONC Cures Act), REG011 (HITRUST), REG012 (No Surprises Act)

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Extended with QBP bonus mechanics, specific measure weights (triple-weighted PDC), HEDIS hybrid measure gaps, and CAHPS percentile thresholds. Master covered general symptoms; subpack adds financial quantification and recovery pathways.

Extended with auto-adjudication sub-causes (benefits configuration, provider data quality, rules engine), call center volume linkage, and first-pass resolution rate detail. Master covered general symptoms; subpack adds payer-specific operational root causes.

Extended with reinsurance cost pressures, COVID-era morbidity unwind dynamics, SG&A benchmarking, and new market entrant competitive pressure. Master covered general MLR symptoms; subpack adds market-specific drivers and segment differentiation.

verticalPersonaAdditions: Chief Medical Officer (Plan) — owns HEDIS/Stars, care gap closure, VBC clinical design
Chief Actuary — owns pricing, reserves, MLR, Stars forecasting, bid optimization
Network Contracting Director — owns network cost, adequacy, disputes, VBC contracts
Grievances & Appeals Manager — owns compliance timelines, overturn rates, CMS sanctions prevention
Population Health Analyst — owns care gap analytics, HEDIS measurement, predictive modeling
Risk Adjustment Director — owns RAF accuracy, HCC recapture, RADV exposure, coding education

HEDIS Hybrid Measure Rate (PK002) — payer-specific hybrid reporting
Medication Adherence PDC (PK003) — triple-weighted Stars measure
CAHPS Health Plan Score (PK004) — plan member experience
Claims Auto-Adjudication Rate (PK008) — payer operational efficiency
FWA Prepay Detection Rate (PK013) — payer fraud prevention maturity
Preventable ED Utilization (PK015) — payer medical cost management
HCC Suspecting Rate (PK019) — payer risk adjustment capture
Digital Engagement Rate (PK022) — payer digital maturity
Network Adequacy Ratio (PK031) — payer network management
COB Recovery Rate (PK046) — payer revenue recovery
DIR Fee Drag (PK049) — payer pharmacy reimbursement
